     The Numbers Business Report

Talladega Nights: The Ballad of Ricky Bobby (2006)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $148,213,377Details
International Box Office $14,657,148
Worldwide Box Office $162,870,525
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $88,507,510
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $675,006
Total $89,182,516
Further financial details...

Synopsis

NASCAR superstar Ricky Bobby is at the top of his game; adored by fans, a trophy wife by his side, and incredible wealth. But Ricky loses it all when French Formula One champion Jean Girard roars onto the scene. Ricky, with the help of his ruthless father, must pull himself out of the depths of despair and restore his honor on the racetrack.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$47,042,215 (31.7% of total gross)
Legs:3.15 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:91.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$73,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,803 opening theaters/3,807 max. theaters, 6.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $270,630,832

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 4th, 2006 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: September 21st, 2006 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: December 12th, 2006 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for crude and sexual humor, language, drug references and brief comic violence
Running Time: 104 minutes
Geographical setting: North Carolina
Plot point: Auto Racing, Comeback, Dysfunctional Family, NASCAR
Source material: Judd Apatow Buddy Comedy
Subgenre: Buddy Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Relativity Media, Judd Apatow, Mosaic Media
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

NASCAR superstar Ricky Bobby is at the top of his game; adored by fans, a trophy wife by his side, and incredible wealth.
